name: Vegetarian Gnocchi with Spinach and Mushroom-Butter Sauce
instructions: 1. Bring a large pot of water to a boil over high heat. Add a generous pinch of salt (about 1 tablespoon) to the water - this will season the gnocchi as they cook. 2. Once the water is at a rolling boil, carefully add the gnocchi. Stir gently to prevent them from sticking to the bottom of the pot. 3. Cook the gnocchi until they float to the surface of the water, which typically takes 2 to 4 minutes. Once they float, they're done - don't overcook or they'll become mushy. 4. Drain the gnocchi in a colander and set aside. Reserve about 1/4 cup of the pasta cooking water in case you need to thin the sauce later. 5. While the gnocchi are cooking, melt the butter in a large saucepan or deep skillet over medium heat. Let it melt completely and begin to foam slightly. 6. Add the sliced cremini mushrooms to the melted butter. Sauté for 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mushrooms are golden brown and have released their moisture. They should be tender and fragrant. 7. Add the minced garlic to the mushrooms and cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly to prevent burning. 8. Toss in the baby spinach, chopped fresh basil, and chopped fresh parsley. Stir everything together - the spinach will seem like a lot at first but will wilt down quickly. 9. Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring frequently, until the spinach is completely wilted and has released its moisture. The mixture should be fragrant and vibrant green. 10. Sprinkle in the grated Parmesan cheese, salt, and black pepper. Stir well to combine. The cheese will melt into the butter and create a light, creamy sauce. If the sauce seems too thick, add a splash of the reserved pasta water. 11. Add the drained gnocchi to the sauce and gently toss to coat each piece evenly. Cook for 2 more minutes, stirring gently, to allow the gnocchi to absorb the flavors and heat through. 12. Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt, pepper, or Parmesan if needed. Serve immediately while hot, garnished with extra Parmesan cheese and fresh basil if desired.
simpleInstructions: 1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook gnocchi until they float, 2-4 minutes. Drain and set aside. 2. Melt butter in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add mushrooms and sauté for 3-4 minutes until golden. 3. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds. 4. Toss in spinach, basil, and parsley. Cook for 2-3 minutes until spinach wilts. 5. Stir in Parmesan cheese, salt, and pepper. 6. Add gnocchi and toss to coat. Cook for 2 more minutes. Serve immediately.
